dublinCore.dc:title: 119 S4044 IS: Support for Astrophysical Observatories and National High-Energy Astrophysics Hubs Act of 2026
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Mr. Markey (for himself and Ms. Warren) introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation

form.official-title: To require the Administrator of the National Aeronautics and Space Administration to designate national high-energy astrophysics hubs.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Support for Astrophysical Observatories and National High-Energy Astrophysics Hubs Act of 2026.

section.header: Support for astrophysical observatories and national high-energy astrophysics hubs

subsection.text: It is the sense of Congress that—

paragraph.text: the United States should maintain its global leadership in high-energy astrophysics;

in order to maintain such leadership, the United States should support X-ray flagship missions based on the recommendations of the most recent and future decadal Surveys in Astronomy and Astrophysics issued by the National Academies of Sciences, Engineering, and Medicine;
the workforce associated with high-energy astrophysics constitutes a national strategic asset that will be critical to the development and eventual operation of any such flagship mission; and

proactive steps should be taken to ensure that the capabilities of current high-energy astrophysics facilities continue to serve the scientific, educational, and commercial interests of the United States long beyond the duration of the current high-energy astrophysics flagship mission.
The Administrator shall designate as a national high-energy astrophysics hub each eligible facility described in paragraph (2).
An eligible facility described in this paragraph is an entity that—

clause.text: is party to a contract with NASA; and
plans and operates missions that conduct activities for purposes of—

subclause.text: ensuring continued United States leadership in high-energy astrophysics and related space sciences;
supporting training and workforce development in data-intensive high-energy astrophysics, aerospace engineering, and spacecraft operations;
advancing United States capabilities in high-performance scientific software, spaceflight operations, and technology transfer;
enabling future high-energy astrophysics missions through mission design, planning, and scientific coordination; or
serving as a collaborative national resource for academic, governmental, and commercial partners; and

clause.text: is an institution of higher education;
is an appropriate State or Federal entity, including a federally funded research and development center; or
is a nongovernmental organization with expertise in advanced energy technology research, development, demonstration, or commercial application.
